name: Alcázar de San Juan and Campo de Criptana Windmills Loop
name_original: Alcázar de San Juan desde Campo de Criptana – circular por Molinos de Viento de San Juan de Alcázar
description: Cycle a moderate 17.6-mile (28.3 km) route past historic windmills and scenic views in Alcázar de San Juan and Campo de Criptana.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Campo De Criptana traverse the expansive La Mancha plain, characterized by its distinctive agricultural landscapes and gentle elevations. The region features low-lying hills such as the Sierra de los Molinos, home to the iconic windmills that provide varied terrain and panoramic views. Cyclists can explore routes winding through vast vineyards and open plains, offering opportunities for both sustained efforts and scenic rides. This area combines historical landmarks with natural features, making it suitable for diverse road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Campo De Criptana The most popular road cycling route is Alcázar de San Juan and Campo de Criptana Windmills Loop, a 17.6 miles (28.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 12 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects two towns and offers views of the historic windmills. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Laguna del Rey – Peñarrolla Reservoir loop from Tomelloso, a moderate 56.6 miles (91.0 km) path. This longer route explores the region's reservoirs and open landscapes. Local road cyclists also love the El Toboso – Ermita de San Isidro loop from Campo de Criptana, a 31.3 miles (50.3 km) trail leading through agricultural plains and small villages, often completed in about 2 hours 8 minutes. Road cycling around Campo De Criptana is defined by expansive plains, low-lying hills, and picturesque vineyards. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides through flat terrain to more challenging routes with gentle climbs. The routes in Campo De Criptana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 17 reviews. More than 500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Campo De Criptana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Campo de Criptana?
A: The region offers a mix of expansive plains and low-lying hills, such as the Sierra de los Molinos. You'll find routes winding through vast vineyards and open agricultural landscapes, providing opportunities for both sustained efforts on flatter sections and gentle climbs with panoramic views.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Campo de Criptana?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes available around Campo de Criptana, catering to various skill levels. These include easy rides through flat terrain, moderate loops, and some more challenging routes with gentle climbs.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy ride?
A: Yes, Campo de Criptana offers several easy road cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38167086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarrolla Reservoir – Tomelloso Chimney loop from Tomelloso&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 34.4 km (21.4 miles) path with minimal elevation gain, perfect for a relaxed ride.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see while cycling?
A: Road cycling routes often feature the iconic windmills of Campo de Criptana, immortalized in Cervantes' *Don Quixote*. You can also explore routes that pass by natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4658585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Veguilla&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Camino de Villafranca&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of the region's wetland complexes.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Campo de Criptana?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 17 reviews. Cyclists often praise the distinctive agricultural landscapes, the picturesque vineyards, and the unique experience of cycling past the historic windmills.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. A popular moderate circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1078779354/alcazar-de-san-juan-and-campo-de-criptana-windmills-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcázar de San Juan and Campo de Criptana Windmills Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 28.3 km (17.6 miles) long and connects two towns while offering views of the historic windmills.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Campo de Criptana?
A: The best time for road cycling in Campo de Criptana is generally during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder and more comfortable for sustained activity. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be chilly, though cycling is possible year-round.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging road cycling routes?
A: For those seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40016128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Rey – Peñarrolla Reservoir loop from Tomelloso&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 91.0 km (56.6 miles) route that explores the region's reservoirs and open landscapes. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24527950" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Antón – Herencia loop from Campo De Criptana&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 78.7 km (48.9 miles) route.

Q: Can I find family-friendly road cycling routes in Campo de Criptana?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations are not always explicit for road cycling, the region's many easy and moderate routes with relatively flat sections, particularly those through the plains, can be suitable for families with older children or those comfortable with road cycling. Always check the route's distance and elevation profile to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay that are cyclist-friendly in Campo de Criptana?
A: Campo de Criptana, being a tourist destination, offers various cafes, restaurants, and accommodations. Many establishments in the town center are accustomed to visitors and can cater to cyclists. It's always a good idea to check in advance for specific bike storage facilities if needed.

Q: Is public transport available to Campo de Criptana for cyclists with bikes?
A: Campo de Criptana is accessible by regional train and bus services. However, policies regarding bicycle carriage can vary significantly by operator and service. It is highly recommended to check with the specific train or bus company beforehand to confirm their rules and any potential fees for transporting bicycles.

Q: Where can I park my car if I'm bringing my bike to Campo de Criptana?
A: Campo de Criptana has various public parking areas within and around the town. You can typically find parking near the main attractions or on the outskirts of the town. Look for designated parking zones, especially if you plan to start your ride directly from the town center.

Q: Are there any permits required for cycling in the Campo de Criptana region?
A: Generally, no special permits are required for road cycling on public roads and established routes in the Campo de Criptana region. However, always respect local regulations, private property, and any specific signage you may encounter, especially if venturing into protected natural areas.
tips: Currently, the Molino Infante, the Burleta, the Culebro, which houses the Sara Montiel Museum, the Chimera, which houses the Museum on Holy Week in the town and the Poyatos Mill, currently a tourist information point, can be visited.

The most characteristic image of Campo de Criptana are the mills that are declared Assets of Cultural Interest, the Infanto, Sardinero and Burleta Mills are in turn declared Monuments of Historical and Artistic Interest.

The Grand Prior's Tower is one of the most emblematic monuments of Alcázar de San Juan (Ciudad Real) and forms part of the Grand Prior's Palace Complex, linked to the Order of Saint John.

Built in the 13th century on the remains of the old Muslim citadel, during the Almohad period.
Reconquest: It became part of the palace of the Grand Priors of Castile and León of the Hospitaller Order of Saint John of Jerusalem.
Expansions: In 1618, under Philip III, a floor was added with windows and plaster ribbed vaults.

The windmills of San Antón Hill are one of the great symbols of Alcázar de San Juan (Ciudad Real) and an essential part of the Don Quixote Route.
They emerged in La Mancha in the mid-16th century as an alternative to the scarcity of watermills, due to the climate and the terrain.
San Antón Hill is a Paleozoic elevation located southeast of the town, about 2.5 km from the town center, with spectacular views of the La Mancha plains.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The estimated moving time for this racebike route is approximately 1 hour and 13 minutes. However, factoring in stops to enjoy the Windmills of Campo de Criptana, the Torreón del Gran Prior, and the Windmills of San Antón Hill, the total duration could be longer, especially if you explore the towns.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily designed for road cycling, meaning you'll encounter mostly paved surfaces like asphalt and paved roads. However, be prepared for some unpaved sections and paths, which might require careful navigation or even dismounting your bike, particularly around the windmill areas.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: This route is rich in historical and scenic landmarks. You'll pass by the iconic Windmills of Campo de Criptana, some dating back to the 16th century, and the Windmills of San Antón Hill in Alcázar de San Juan, famously named after Don Quixote characters. You can also see the Torreón del Gran Prior and a Roman bridge over the Záncara River.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is rated as 'moderate' in difficulty. While it's a racebike route, the varied terrain with potential unpaved sections means it's best suited for cyclists with good fitness and some experience. Beginners might find certain segments challenging.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to cycle in La Mancha, offering pleasant temperatures and avoiding the intense heat of summer. These seasons also provide beautiful light for photography of the windmills and landscapes.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this cycling route?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in both Alcázar de San Juan and Campo de Criptana. As this is a circular route, you can start from either town and park in designated public parking areas. Specific parking lots for cyclists are not explicitly mentioned, but town centers usually offer options.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. The roads and paths are publicly accessible. However, individual attractions like museums within the towns or specific windmill interiors might have their own entry fees.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route primarily follows public roads and paths, which are generally accessible, it's a racebike route. Cycling with dogs, especially on a leash, can be challenging and potentially unsafe on roads. If you plan to bring a dog, consider if they can safely keep pace or if a trailer is appropriate, and always ensure they are under control.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Alcázar de San Juan and Campo de Criptana are towns with various cafes, restaurants, and pubs where you can refuel before, during, or after your ride. It's advisable to carry water and snacks for the cycling sections between towns.

faqs.question: Can I access the starting points by public transport with my bike?
faqs.answer: Both Alcázar de San Juan and Campo de Criptana are well-connected by public transport, including train services. However, policies regarding bringing bicycles on trains or buses can vary. It's recommended to check with the specific transport provider (e.g., Renfe for trains) beforehand to confirm their bicycle carriage rules and any associated fees or restrictions.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with several other local paths. You'll find yourself on small sections of trails such as Camino Natural de los Humedales de la Mancha, Ruta de los pozos y del agua, Camino de los Siete Molinos, and Camino de Manzanares, among others.
